description: Kernel Architecture & WasmVM Integration — restructure secure-exec into a kernel-based OS with pluggable runtime drivers and migrate WasmVM into the monorepo

userStories.title: Migrate WasmVM Rust workspace into monorepo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WasmVM Rust workspace migrated from ~/seos/wasmcore into wasmvm/ in the secure-exec monorepo so all code lives in one repository.
acceptanceCriteria: wasmvm/ directory exists with Cargo.toml workspace definition, crates/ (multicall, shims, wasi-ext), stubs/, patches/, scripts/
wasmvm/CLAUDE.md exists with WasmVM-specific build, deps, and patching instructions
wasmvm/.gitignore ignores target/ and vendor/
Root .gitignore includes wasmvm/target/ and wasmvm/vendor/
turbo.json has build:wasm task with inputs wasmvm/**/*.rs and output wasmvm/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/multicall.opt.wasm
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate WasmVM TypeScript host into monorepo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WasmVM TypeScript host migrated from ~/seos/wasmcore/host into packages/runtime/wasmvm/ so the TS host is a proper monorepo package.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/wasmvm/ exists with package.json, tsconfig.json, src/, test/
pnpm-workspace.yaml includes packages/runtime/*
pnpm install succeeds with the new package
All existing WasmVM TypeScript tests pass (pnpm --filter @secure-exec/wasmvm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate WasmVM docs and update monorepo metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WasmVM documentation migrated and monorepo docs updated with kernel terminology so all project context is consolidated.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/compatibility-matrix.md migrated from ~/seos/docs/
docs/prior-art.md migrated from ~/seos/docs/
wasmvm/notes/ contains all migrated internal docs (todo.md, mvp-blockers.md, specs/, research/)
docs-internal/glossary.md includes definitions for Kernel, Runtime Driver, WasmVM, VFS, FD Table, Process Table, Command Registry, Device Layer
docs-internal/arch/overview.md updated with kernel architecture diagram and all component descriptions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All artifacts already in place from commit b7a78c4

userStories.title: Create kernel package with types and VFS interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the kernel package scaffolded with all core type definitions and the POSIX-complete VFS interface so other kernel components can build on them.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/package.json exists with name @secure-exec/kernel
packages/kernel/tsconfig.json configured

packages/kernel/src/types.ts defines: Kernel, KernelOptions, ExecOptions, ExecResult, SpawnOptions, ManagedProcess, RuntimeDriver, ProcessContext, DriverProcess, KernelInterface, FD types (FDStat, FileDescription, FDEntry, O_* flags, SEEK_*, FILETYPE_*), Process types (ProcessEntry, ProcessInfo, SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGINT), Pipe types, Permission types (FsAccessRequest with symlink/readlink/link/chmod/chown/utimes/truncate ops)

packages/kernel/src/vfs.ts defines VirtualFileSystem interface with: readFile, writeFile, mkdir, readdir, stat, unlink, rename, rmdir, realpath, exists, symlink, readlink, lstat, link, chmod, chown, utimes, truncate
VirtualStat extended with isSymbolicLink, ino, nlink, uid, gid
packages/kernel/src/index.ts exports all public types
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Kernel types and VFS from commit b7a78c4; added missing realpath to VFS interface and all implementations

userStories.title: Implement kernel FD table with shared FileDescriptions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-PID file descriptor tables with shared cursor positions via dup/dup2 so processes can share file state correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/src/fd-table.ts implements ProcessFDTable with open, openWith, get, close, dup, dup2, stat, fork, closeAll
FDTableManager creates per-PID tables with stdio FDs (0, 1, 2) pre-allocated
FDTableManager supports fork (copies parent FDs), get, and remove
Shared FileDescription objects have refcounting — closing one FD does not affect duplicates
Cursor position is shared across dup'd FDs
Tests in packages/kernel/test/fd-table.test.ts pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement kernel process table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a process table that tracks all processes across runtimes with PID allocation, parent-child relationships, waitpid, and signal routing.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/src/process-table.ts implements ProcessTable with register, get, markExited, waitpid, kill, getppid, listProcesses, terminateAll
Sequential PID allocation starting from 1
Parent-child relationship tracking
Waiter-based waitpid with Promise resolution when process exits
Zombie process cleanup after ZOMBIE_TTL_MS (60s)
Tests in packages/kernel/test/process-table.test.ts pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement kernel device layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the device layer to intercept /dev/* paths before they reach the VFS backend so device nodes work consistently across runtimes.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/src/device-layer.ts implements createDeviceLayer(vfs) wrapping a VFS
Handles /dev/null (EOF read, discard write), /dev/zero (zero-fill read), /dev/urandom (random bytes)
Handles /dev/stdin, /dev/stdout, /dev/stderr
EPERM on remove/rename of device nodes
Non-device paths pass through to backing VFS unchanged
Tests in packages/kernel/test/device-layer.test.ts pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement kernel command registry and pipe manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need command→driver routing and cross-runtime pipe management so the kernel can orchestrate multi-process pipelines.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/src/command-registry.ts implements CommandRegistry with register, resolve, list, populateBin
populateBin creates stub files in /bin for shell PATH lookup
Last-mounted driver wins on command name conflict
packages/kernel/src/pipe-manager.ts implements PipeManager with createPipe, write, read (async with blocking), close, isPipe, createPipeFDs
EOF propagation when write end closes
drainBuffer reads multiple chunks up to requested length
Tests in packages/kernel/test/command-registry.test.ts and pipe-manager.test.ts pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement kernel permissions, user, and entry point
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deny-by-default permissions, user identity, and the main kernel factory so the kernel is fully functional.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/src/permissions.ts implements wrapFileSystem() wrapping all VFS ops with permission checks
Permission presets: allowAllFs, allowAllNetwork, allowAllChildProcess, allowAllEnv, allowAll
packages/kernel/src/user.ts implements UserManager with uid, gid, euid, egid, username, homedir, shell, getpwuid
packages/kernel/src/kernel.ts implements KernelImpl with mount(), dispose(), exec(), spawn()
createKernel(options) factory function returns a Kernel
exec() routes through shell ('sh' command from registry)
createKernelInterface() builds the syscall surface passed to drivers on mount
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create OS node package with NodeFileSystem and NodeWorkerAdapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Node.js platform adapters implementing the kernel's VFS and worker interfaces so the kernel runs on Node.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/os/node/ exists with package.json (@secure-exec/os-node), tsconfig.json
packages/os/node/src/filesystem.ts implements VirtualFileSystem by delegating to node:fs/promises
packages/os/node/src/worker.ts wraps node:worker_threads as a WorkerAdapter
packages/os/node/src/index.ts exports NodeFileSystem and NodeWorkerAdapter
pnpm-workspace.yaml includes packages/os/*
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create OS browser package with InMemoryFileSystem
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a POSIX-complete in-memory filesystem and browser worker adapter so the kernel runs in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/os/browser/ exists with package.json (@secure-exec/os-browser), tsconfig.json
packages/os/browser/src/filesystem.ts implements InMemoryFileSystem with symlinks, hard links, permissions metadata, inode tracking
InMemoryFileSystem implements full VirtualFileSystem interface including symlink, readlink, lstat, link, chmod, chown, utimes, truncate
packages/os/browser/src/worker.ts wraps Web Worker API as a WorkerAdapter
packages/os/browser/src/index.ts exports InMemoryFileSystem and BrowserWorkerAdapter
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Thin WasmVM WASI polyfill file I/O to use kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WASI file I/O syscalls (fd_read, fd_write, path_open, fd_seek, fd_close, fd_prestat_get, fd_prestat_dir_name) to delegate to the kernel's FD table and VFS instead of WasmVM's standalone implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: WASI polyfill fd_read delegates to kernel fdRead via KernelInterface
WASI polyfill fd_write delegates to kernel fdWrite via KernelInterface
WASI polyfill path_open delegates to kernel fdOpen via KernelInterface
WASI polyfill fd_seek delegates to kernel fdSeek via KernelInterface
WASI polyfill fd_close delegates to kernel fdClose via KernelInterface
WasmVM's standalone file I/O logic in the polyfill is removed
Existing WasmVM file I/O tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Thin WasmVM WASI polyfill process and FD management to kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WASI process syscalls (proc_exit, args_get, args_sizes_get, environ_get, environ_sizes_get) and FD stat/management to use kernel process and FD tables.
acceptanceCriteria: WASI polyfill proc_exit delegates to kernel process table markExited
WASI polyfill args_get/args_sizes_get use ProcessContext from kernel
WASI polyfill environ_get/environ_sizes_get use ProcessContext env from kernel
WASI polyfill fd_fdstat_get delegates to kernel fdStat via KernelInterface
WasmVM's standalone process/FD management logic removed from polyfill
Existing WasmVM process and env tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Delete WasmVM standalone VFS, FD table, process table, and pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WasmVM's standalone OS-layer code deleted now that all operations delegate to the kernel, eliminating duplicated state.
acceptanceCriteria: WasmVM's standalone vfs.ts (internal VFS implementation) is deleted
WasmVM's standalone FD table logic is deleted
WasmVM's standalone process table logic is deleted
WasmVM's standalone device node handling is deleted (moved to kernel device layer)
WasmVM's /bin population logic is deleted (moved to kernel command registry)
WasmVM's pipeline.ts is deleted (kernel coordinates pipelines)
No standalone OS-layer state remains in WasmVM — all goes through kernel
All WasmVM tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Adapt WasmVM tests from node:test to vitest
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WasmVM tests converted from node:test to vitest so they integrate with the monorepo's test runner.
acceptanceCriteria: All test files in packages/runtime/wasmvm/test/ use vitest imports (describe, it, expect) instead of node:test (test, assert)
vitest.config.ts or vitest config in package.json configured for the package
pnpm --filter @secure-exec/wasmvm test runs all tests via vitest
All converted tests pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create WasmVM RuntimeDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createWasmVmRuntime() factory that returns a RuntimeDriver so WasmVM can mount into the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/wasmvm/src/driver.ts exports createWasmVmRuntime() returning a RuntimeDriver
RuntimeDriver.name is 'wasmvm'
RuntimeDriver.commands lists all 90+ commands (sh, bash, grep, sed, awk, cat, ls, etc.)
RuntimeDriver.init(kernel) stores KernelInterface reference for syscalls
RuntimeDriver.spawn(command, args, ctx) creates a Worker, loads WASM binary, runs command, returns DriverProcess with pid, exitCode promise, stdin/stdout/stderr write/onData, and kill
RuntimeDriver.dispose() terminates all active workers
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create Node RuntimeDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createNodeRuntime() factory that wraps the existing Node execution driver behind the RuntimeDriver interface so Node can mount into the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/node/src/driver.ts exports createNodeRuntime(options) returning a RuntimeDriver
RuntimeDriver.name is 'node'
RuntimeDriver.commands lists ['node', 'npm', 'npx']
RuntimeDriver.init(kernel) stores KernelInterface, configures bridge to use kernel VFS/FD/process
RuntimeDriver.spawn(command, args, ctx) creates an isolate, runs code, returns DriverProcess
RuntimeDriver.dispose() disposes all active isolates
Existing secure-exec Node tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create Python RuntimeDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createPythonRuntime() factory that wraps the existing Pyodide driver behind the RuntimeDriver interface so Python can mount into the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/python/src/driver.ts exports createPythonRuntime(options) returning a RuntimeDriver
RuntimeDriver.name is 'python'
RuntimeDriver.commands lists ['python', 'python3', 'pip']
RuntimeDriver.init(kernel) stores KernelInterface, configures Pyodide worker to use kernel VFS
RuntimeDriver.spawn(command, args, ctx) creates a Pyodide worker, runs code, returns DriverProcess
RuntimeDriver.dispose() terminates Pyodide worker
Existing secure-exec Python tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire kernel.exec() end-to-end with mounted runtimes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need kernel.exec() to work end-to-end: parse command, look up in registry, spawn via the correct runtime driver, capture output, return ExecResult.
acceptanceCriteria: kernel.exec('echo hello') returns { code: 0, stdout: 'hello\n', stderr: '' } when WasmVM is mounted
kernel.exec('ls /tmp') works when files exist in kernel VFS
kernel.exec('cat /tmp/test.txt') returns file contents from kernel VFS
kernel.exec('nonexistent-command') returns non-zero exit code with error on stderr
kernel.spawn('echo', ['hello']) returns ManagedProcess with working stdout onData and exitCode
Integration test verifying createKernel + mount(wasmvmDriver) + exec works
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Route Node bridge child_process.spawn through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Node bridge's child_process.spawn polyfill to route through the kernel's command registry instead of the host's child_process, enabling cross-runtime command execution from Node code.
acceptanceCriteria: Node bridge child_process.spawn('ls', ['/tmp']) resolves via kernel command registry to WasmVM runtime
Node bridge child_process.execSync('echo hello') works through kernel
Spawned processes get proper PIDs from kernel process table
stdout/stderr from spawned processes are piped back to the Node caller
Commands not in the registry return ENOENT-like error
Existing secure-exec child_process tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement cross-runtime pipes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cross-runtime pipelines to work so commands like 'echo hello | node -e "process.stdin.pipe(process.stdout)"' execute correctly across WasmVM and Node runtimes.
acceptanceCriteria: kernel.exec('echo hello | cat') works (WasmVM-to-WasmVM pipe)
kernel.exec('echo hello | node -e "..."') pipes WasmVM stdout to Node stdin when both runtimes mounted
kernel.exec('cat file | python -c "..."') pipes WasmVM stdout to Python stdin when both runtimes mounted
Pipe EOF propagation: when writer closes, reader gets EOF
PipeManager createPipeFDs allocates FDs in the kernel FD table for both ends
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement FD inheritance for child processes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need child processes to inherit their parent's file descriptor table so redirections and pipes work correctly across process boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: When kernel.spawn forks a process, child gets a copy of parent's FD table via FDTableManager.fork
Inherited FDs share cursor position with parent (via shared FileDescription refcounting)
Child can close inherited FDs without affecting parent
Redirections (e.g., '> /tmp/out.txt') modify child's FD table before exec
Integration test: parent opens file, spawns child, child reads from inherited FD
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement signal forwarding across runtimes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need kill(pid, signal) to work across runtimes so processes can be terminated regardless of which runtime owns them.
acceptanceCriteria: kernel process table kill(pid, SIGTERM) routes to the correct runtime driver's DriverProcess.kill()
SIGTERM gives the process a chance to clean up before termination
SIGKILL immediately terminates the process
kill() on a non-existent PID returns ESRCH error
Signal delivery works across runtimes (Node process can kill a WasmVM process and vice versa)
Integration test: spawn a long-running process, send SIGTERM, verify it exits
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create kernel behavioral contracts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need behavioral contracts for the kernel package so future changes are reviewed against explicit requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: .agent/contracts/kernel.md exists defining behavioral requirements for: VFS interface semantics, FD table open/close/dup/dup2/fork, process table register/waitpid/kill/zombie cleanup, device layer intercepts and EPERM rules, pipe manager blocking read/EOF/drain, command registry resolution and /bin population, permission deny-by-default wrapping

.agent/contracts/kernel-runtime-driver.md exists defining: RuntimeDriver interface contract (init/spawn/dispose lifecycle), command registration rules, ProcessContext requirements, DriverProcess exit/kill/stdio contract
Both contracts use Requirement/Scenario/WHEN/THEN format consistent with existing contracts
.agent/contracts/README.md updated with new contract entries in the index table
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update existing contracts for kernel model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need existing contracts updated to reflect the kernel-mediated execution model so contracts remain the authoritative source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: .agent/contracts/node-runtime.md updated: execution goes through kernel mount + spawn, not standalone driver construction
.agent/contracts/node-bridge.md updated: child_process.spawn routes through kernel command registry
.agent/contracts/node-permissions.md updated: documents interaction between kernel-level permissions (wrapFileSystem) and existing secure-exec permission wrappers
.agent/contracts/runtime-driver-integration-testing.md updated: test infrastructure supports kernel-aware TestContext with mounted drivers
.agent/contracts/runtime-driver-test-suite-structure.md updated: adds kernel test patterns (kernel unit tests in packages/kernel/test/, cross-runtime integration tests)
.agent/contracts/compatibility-governance.md updated: cross-runtime parity requirements (same VFS/FD/process state across WasmVM, Node, Python)
All contract changes are internally consistent and use existing WHEN/THEN format
Typecheck passes
